High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Database reviewer note: doxorubicin: anthracycline cardiac toxicity compounded by concurrent cardiac RT dose. Boekel: anthracycline+trastuzumab HF RR 34.9; V20>=20% compounds risk. Herrmann: anthracyclines primary cardiomyopathy mechanism. Mitchell: RT+anthracycline = major combined cardiovascular risk. Cumulative anthracycline dose (>450mg/m2 doxorubicin equivalent) critical threshold.

High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Database reviewer note: PI/class review: CRS and ICANS risk; concurrent RT to CNS/marrow during CRS/ICANS phase: extreme caution. No published RT-interaction data. No RT-specific interaction evidence found. Retain as insufficient evidence pending dedicated research. Web search confirmed: no published concurrent RT-interaction data for this agent. PI-based CRS/ICANS/marrow characterisation retained. Retain insufficient evidence. Consensus meta-analysis (Liu/Tai/Ramteke 2024-25): pooled BsAb cardiovascular AEs - tachycardia 10.2%, arrhythmia 12.6%, hypotension 13.3%. Teclistamab: myocarditis signal (ROR 25.70). ChatGPT literature search 2026-06-13 confirmed: no published concurrent RT-interaction clinical data for this agent. Classification confirmed correct.

High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.RT toxicity domains: CRS/ICANS (timing-critical: avoid RT during active CRS/ICANS); bridging RT before CAR-T: safe and may REDUCE severe CRS; concurrent RT during CAR-T infusion period: no data; post-CAR-T RT: under investigation
High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Highest-concern RT sites / OARs: CNS (ICANS + brain RT); marrow-rich fields (prolonged cytopenias)
High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Fractionation-specific warning: Avoid unreviewed concurrent RT; urgent/palliative RT may be appropriate with specialist coordination
High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Timing-specific warning: CRITICAL DISTINCTION: (1) Bridging RT BEFORE CAR-T (days 10-25 prior): evidence supports safety, may reduce severe CRS grade >=3 vs chemo bridging. (2) Concurrent RT DURING CRS/ICANS (around infusion day 0): avoid - no data, high risk. (3) Post-CAR-T RT: under investigation. Lymphodepletion chemo (Cy/Flu) given days -5 to -3 before infusion compounds marrow suppression.

High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Database reviewer note: CD19 CAR-T: PI: CRS grades 1-4; ICANS (neurological). Prolonged cytopenias post-infusion may last months. RT timing relative to CAR-T: no published evidence. Lymphodepletion conditioning (typically cyclophosphamide+fludarabine given 5-7 days prior) is itself RT-relevant. CNS RT post-CAR-T ICANS: extreme caution. Specialist (haematology + radiation oncology) planning essential. ASTRO 2024 pilot: split-course RT as bridging before CAR-T (after lymphodepletion) reported feasible in early data (Imber et al, NCT06104592); RT-as-bridging is distinct from concurrent RT during CRS. Post-CAR-T RT: pilot study ongoing (NCT04473937). No safety data extractable from abstracts at this time.

High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Database reviewer note: ATRA for APML. PI: differentiation syndrome (boxed); pseudotumour cerebri (raised ICP) - relevant for concurrent brain/cranial RT; QTc prolongation. Preclinical: ATRA + RT enhances tumour radioresponse via inflammatory macrophages / immune modulation (Rao 2021, Science Immunology); suggests tumour radiosensitisation rather than normal-tissue injury increase. NO clinical concurrent-RT toxicity study in APML. RT rarely used in contemporary APML management. APML-specific risks dominate: differentiation syndrome, bleeding (thrombocytopenia), leukocytosis, hepatotoxicity, ATO co-administration. Do not infer clinical safety from experimental models. Retain Insufficient evidence.

High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Database reviewer note: IDH1 inhibitor for AML/glioma/CCA; PI: QTc prolongation boxed warning, differentiation syndrome (19% DS rate in AML, 6% fatal). Glioma: ivosidenib phase I (Mellinghoff 2020) - used in RT-naive patients as ALTERNATIVE to RT, or post-RT sequentially; no concurrent RT arm. Phase I PK study excluded RT within 21 days. Clinical practice: IDHi in low-grade glioma is used INSTEAD of or AFTER RT (vorasidenib INDIGO trial: RT-naive grade 2 glioma). Concurrent RT + IDH inhibitor in glioma not established; differentiation syndrome + brain RT: high-risk combination with uncertain safety. Retain Insufficient evidence - upgrade from generic placeholder to evidence-contextualised. ChatGPT literature search 2026-06-13 confirmed: no published concurrent RT-interaction clinical data for this agent. Classification confirmed correct.

High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Database reviewer note: ICI+RT: overall G3-4 comparable to ICI alone (Sha: 16.3% vs 22.3%); lung SBRT+ICI grade 3 pneumonitis 10.7% vs 0% (Tian; P<0.01); ICI prior to CRT: RP 45.3% vs 25% consolidation (Qin - timing critical); prior irAE: 83% RP if prior pneumonitis irAE (Shaverdian); RRP: 18.8% incidence, median 450 days post-RT (Cousin); PACIFIC 5yr: G3/4 30.5% durvalumab vs 26.1% placebo. Radiation recall pneumonitis: distinct pattern, up to 5yr post-RT. Class: CTLA-4 (ipilimumab) - colitis dominant; PD-1/PD-L1 - pneumonitis dominant (Wang fatal ICI data).

High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Database reviewer note: ICI+RT: overall G3-4 comparable to ICI alone (Sha: 16.3% vs 22.3%); lung SBRT+ICI grade 3 pneumonitis 10.7% vs 0% (Tian; P<0.01); ICI prior to CRT: RP 45.3% vs 25% consolidation (Qin - timing critical); prior irAE: 83% RP if prior pneumonitis irAE (Shaverdian); RRP: 18.8% incidence, median 450 days post-RT (Cousin); PACIFIC 5yr: G3/4 30.5% durvalumab vs 26.1% placebo. Radiation recall pneumonitis: distinct pattern, up to 5yr post-RT. Class: CTLA-4 (ipilimumab) - colitis dominant; PD-1/PD-L1 - pneumonitis dominant (Wang fatal ICI data).

High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.RT toxicity domains: Pneumonitis, colitis/enteritis, hepatitis, dermatitis, endocrinopathy, inflammatory flare/radiation recall
High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Highest-concern RT sites / OARs: Lung, bowel/rectum, liver, skin, brain oedema/radionecrosis context
High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Fractionation-specific warning: Caution with thoracic RT, abdominal/pelvic RT, brain SRS/SABR and large-field hypofractionation
High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Timing-specific warning: ICI BEFORE CRT: highest pneumonitis risk (45.3%); ICI concurrent: 24.3%; consolidation: 25.3%. Prior pneumonitis irAE: avoid thoracic RT if possible or monitor closely. Radiation recall pneumonitis: can occur up to 5 years after RT.
High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Medical Oncology discussion: Yes for thorax/abdomen/pelvis/brain SRS or symptomatic immune toxicity
High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Senior RO review: Yes for high-dose thoracic RT, brain SRS or reirradiation contexts

High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Database reviewer note: PACIFIC 5yr (EVID-0031): consolidation durvalumab after CRT - G3/4 30.5% vs 26.1%; 15.4% discontinuation. Any-grade pneumonitis 33.9%; G3-4 3.4%; 9.5% discontinued for pneumonitis. Timing: ICI before CRT highest pneumonitis risk (Qin). RRP up to 5yr post-RT. Standard of care for stage III NSCLC; monitoring protocol essential.

High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Database reviewer note: Direct olaparib+RT: H&N severe DLTs at higher dose/accelerated (Navran); STS G3 dermatitis 34% + 4.8% G5, avoid critical vasculature (Sargos); thoracic pulmonary toxicity; GBM/breast tolerable at moderate fractionation. MTD H&N olaparib 25mg QD. Macchia 2025 (EVID-0035): concurrent PARPi+SBRT for ovarian oligoprogression - only 2.7% G3+ acute toxicity; supports SBRT feasibility with PARPi at standard ablative doses. Contrast with H&N/STS higher-risk scenarios.

High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Database reviewer note: Cochrane IPD meta (EVID-0041): CRT 6% OS benefit, significantly increased haematologic/GI toxicity. Szturz: weekly low-dose cisplatin significantly less G3-4 myelosuppression/nephrotoxicity vs 3-weekly 100mg/m2 (P<0.001). Konnerth + Rose: pelvic BM V10<95%, V20<76% reduce G3+ leukopenia. Wang oesophageal: concurrent CRT - pneumonitis and oesophagitis grade>=3 dose-dependent. Verma: NSCLC concurrent CRT oesophagitis and pneumonitis primary toxicities.

High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Change status: Upgraded from Insufficient evidence to Caution - concurrent RT data found
High_Risk_Master_217_FIXED.Database reviewer note: Lim 2025 (The Oncologist): multicenter retrospective - concurrent local therapy (liver-directed + RT) added to tebentafusp at progression feasible with manageable safety. RT-specific toxicity not separately quantified. CRS highest in first 3 doses. Uveal melanoma context; RT to liver/soft tissue.

RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_toxicity_domains: Neurologic; skeletal; GI; neuropathy; skin; marrow
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Notes: Add pharynx/larynx for upper cervical fields.

RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_inferred_OARs: Spinal cord; vertebral body; lungs; oesophagus; skin; marrow
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_toxicity_domains: Neurologic; skeletal; pulmonary; GI; skin; marrow
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Notes: Level-dependent thoracic OARs should be selectable.

RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_inferred_OARs: Cord/cauda equina; vertebral body; bowel; kidneys; skin; marrow
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_toxicity_domains: Neurologic; skeletal; GI; renal; skin; marrow
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Notes: Cauda equina and bowel are commonly relevant.

RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_inferred_OARs: Liver; stomach; duodenum; bowel; kidneys
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_toxicity_domains: Hepatic; GI; renal; biliary
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Notes: Segment/location determines stomach, duodenum and bowel emphasis.

RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_inferred_OARs: Pancreas; stomach; duodenum; bowel; liver; kidneys
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_toxicity_domains: GI; hepatic; renal; biliary; neurologic
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Notes: Duodenum and stomach are usually major limiting OARs.

RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_inferred_OARs: Kidneys; bowel; stomach; liver; spinal cord
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_toxicity_domains: Renal; GI; hepatic; neurologic; pulmonary
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Notes: Laterality and target size determine adjacent OARs.

RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_inferred_OARs: Skin; subcutaneous tissue; wound bed
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_toxicity_domains: Dermatitis; wound healing; necrosis; pain
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Notes: Add cartilage, bone or mucosa where anatomically relevant.

RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_inferred_OARs: Bone; marrow; skin
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Optional_context_OARs: Joint; bowel; bladder; lung; neurovascular structures
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_toxicity_domains: Pain flare; fracture; marrow; skin; site-specific organ toxicity
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Notes: Automatically add adjacent organs according to location.

RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_inferred_OARs: Clinician-selected
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Default_toxicity_domains: Clinician-selected
RT_Site_Subsite_OARs.Notes: Requires explicit manual OAR and toxicity selection.

Fractionation_Categories.Key_interaction_implications: Cumulative mucosal, marrow and organ toxicity; prolonged overlap with systemic therapy
Fractionation_Categories.Senior_RO_trigger: No unless other drivers elevate risk

Fractionation_Categories.Key_interaction_implications: Greater per-fraction normal-tissue effect; consider radiosensitising agents and late effects
Higher per-fraction risk to serial organs; limited concurrent systemic evidence
Fractionation_Categories.Senior_RO_trigger: Yes when critical OAR overlap or uncertain agent interaction

Fractionation_Categories.Key_interaction_implications: High-dose focal injury, vascular/airway/GI/chest-wall risk; avoid assumptions from conventional RT
Fractionation_Categories.Senior_RO_trigger: Yes for central/ultracentral or high-risk agent

Fractionation_Categories.Rule_definition: Ablative intracranial treatment, usually single or few fractions
Fractionation_Categories.Key_interaction_implications: Radionecrosis/oedema risk; caution with agents linked to vascular injury, immune activation or impaired healing
Short overlap but high single-fraction effect; site-specific toxicity usually dominates
Fractionation_Categories.Senior_RO_trigger: Only if critical OAR or high-risk agent

Fractionation_Categories.Key_interaction_implications: Marrow/GI/skin overlap depends on field; often feasible with selective holds
Fractionation_Categories.Senior_RO_trigger: Consider for large fields or marrow-suppressive therapy

Fractionation_Categories.Rule_definition: Pelvis, spine, craniospinal, extended nodes or other marrow-rich fields
Fractionation_Categories.Key_interaction_implications: Additive cytopenia risk with myelosuppressive agents; blood count monitoring and treatment coordination
Fractionation_Categories.Senior_RO_trigger: Yes if severe baseline cytopenia or intensive therapy

Fractionation_Categories.Rule_definition: Any fractionation in previously irradiated tissue
Fractionation_Categories.Key_interaction_implications: Outside core app scope for dose summation; interaction warnings still apply but cumulative dose requires specialist assessment
App calculates category from dose/fraction where possible; clinician confirms
Fractionation_Categories.Senior_RO_trigger: Based on calculated category and OAR overlap

Timing_Definitions.Operational_definition: Systemic therapy administered during the RT course or on an RT treatment day
Timing_Definitions.Required_user_input: Last dose date; next dose date; schedule
Timing_Definitions.Default_app_interpretation: Highest likelihood of overlapping acute toxicity or radiosensitisation
Timing_Definitions.Escalation_logic: Apply agent-specific concurrent warning and combination rules

Timing_Definitions.Operational_definition: Systemic therapy stopped before RT but residual pharmacologic/biologic effect may remain
Timing_Definitions.Default_app_interpretation: Use agent-specific washout when available; otherwise classify by half-life/biologic persistence
Timing_Definitions.Escalation_logic: Escalate if within known hold interval, unresolved toxicity or long biologic effect

Timing_Definitions.Operational_definition: Systemic therapy planned soon after RT while acute tissue injury is evolving
Timing_Definitions.Default_app_interpretation: Assess wound healing, marrow recovery, mucosal recovery and radiation recall potential
Timing_Definitions.Escalation_logic: Escalate if restart precedes expected recovery or agent has recall/wound-healing concern

Timing_Definitions.Operational_definition: Systemic therapy and RT separated beyond immediate overlap interval
Timing_Definitions.Default_app_interpretation: Usually lower concern, but late recall and persistent organ toxicity remain possible
Timing_Definitions.Escalation_logic: Apply residual-risk warning where agent has long half-life or delayed toxicity

Timing_Definitions.Operational_definition: Systemic therapy not yet started but intended after RT
Timing_Definitions.Default_app_interpretation: Generate restart/start guidance and required monitoring
Timing_Definitions.Escalation_logic: Escalate for anti-VEGF, strong myelosuppression, immune toxicity or wound-healing concern

Timing_Definitions.Default_app_interpretation: Do not downgrade risk; show uncertainty warning
Timing_Definitions.Escalation_logic: Require clinician confirmation before final output

Interaction_Rules.RT_site_or_OAR_trigger: Pelvic marrow; spine; craniospinal; extended nodal fields
Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Check FBC; assess field size and marrow burden; consider treatment hold or schedule adjustment
Interaction_Rules.Senior_RO_review: If severe cytopenia or intensive regimen
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: Potential additive marrow suppression from systemic therapy and marrow-exposing RT.

Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Discuss temporary interruption; monitor FBC and GI toxicity
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: Concurrent CDK4/6 inhibition may increase marrow and gastrointestinal toxicity, particularly with pelvic or abdominal RT.

Interaction_Rules.RT_site_or_OAR_trigger: Bowel; stomach; duodenum; oesophagus; central lung; major vessels; wound bed
Interaction_Rules.Fractionation_trigger: SABR/SBRT or high dose per fraction
Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Avoid concurrent treatment unless justified. For SBRT/SABR, coordinate an agent-specific hold; the Sanford framework suggests considering 1–2 weeks before RT, but longer intervals may be required according to drug half-life, site and stronger evidence.
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: Anti-VEGF pathway inhibition may increase bleeding, perforation, fistula, vascular injury and impaired healing with high-dose RT near hollow viscera or major vessels.

Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Assess baseline pneumonitis risk; monitor respiratory symptoms; do not automatically withhold when clinically indicated
Interaction_Rules.Senior_RO_review: For prior pneumonitis, ILD or ultracentral SABR
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: Potential overlapping immune-mediated and radiation pneumonitis risk.

Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Monitor oedema/radionecrosis; review steroid requirement and lesion volume
Interaction_Rules.Senior_RO_review: For large lesions, prior radionecrosis or uncertain symptoms
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: SRS with immune checkpoint therapy may increase inflammatory imaging changes, oedema or radionecrosis in selected settings.

Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Review ILD history; consider treatment separation; monitor for pneumonitis
Interaction_Rules.Senior_RO_review: Yes if prior ILD or substantial lung dose
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: Potential additive interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis risk with thoracic RT.

Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Consider temporary interruption according to agent/class guidance; monitor pneumonitis
Interaction_Rules.Senior_RO_review: Recommended for SABR or pre-existing ILD
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: Concurrent thoracic RT with targeted kinase inhibition may increase pulmonary toxicity.

Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Use agent-specific guidance. Sanford reports: SBRT/SRS—hold during RT and 1 day before/after; conventional/palliative RT—hold during RT and 3 days before/after. Verify against current consensus, drug pharmacology and clinical urgency.
Interaction_Rules.Senior_RO_review: For SABR/SRS or visceral high-dose RT
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: BRAF/MEK inhibition may enhance radiation dermatitis or recall and has been associated with visceral toxicity and radionecrosis.

Interaction_Rules.RT_site_or_OAR_trigger: Head and neck; oesophagus; pelvis; abdomen
Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Use protocol-specific regimen and supportive care; app should not label protocol-standard therapy as avoid
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: This is a recognised concurrent chemoradiation combination; toxicity risk is expected and should be managed according to the relevant protocol.

Interaction_Rules.RT_site_or_OAR_trigger: Head and neck; oesophagus; bowel; rectum; anal canal
Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Increase mucosal/GI monitoring and supportive care; assess whether concurrent use is intentional
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: Concurrent systemic therapy may intensify mucosal toxicity in the irradiated field.

Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Document baseline pulmonary status; monitor symptoms and imaging; consider separation for high-risk agents
Interaction_Rules.Senior_RO_review: Yes for prior ILD or high lung dose
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: The selected systemic therapy and thoracic RT have overlapping pneumonitis/ILD risk.

Interaction_Rules.Agent_or_class_trigger: Any agent associated with severe diarrhoea/colitis
Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Assess baseline bowel symptoms; monitor hydration and stool frequency; consider treatment separation
Interaction_Rules.Senior_RO_review: If severe baseline symptoms or high bowel dose
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: Potential additive gastrointestinal toxicity from systemic therapy and bowel irradiation.

Interaction_Rules.Timing_trigger: During active CRS/ICANS or unresolved immune toxicity
Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Defer non-urgent RT during active CRS/ICANS; urgent RT requires multidisciplinary review
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: Avoid elective RT during active cytokine-release syndrome, ICANS or unstable immune toxicity.

Interaction_Rules.RT_site_or_OAR_trigger: Skin; postoperative bed; ulcerated tumour; bowel
Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Coordinate hold interval and wound review; avoid high-dose RT to unstable wounds when possible
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: Systemic therapy may impair wound healing and increase tissue breakdown in the irradiated region.

Interaction_Rules.Agent_or_class_trigger: Any agent with no direct RT evidence
Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Use class/mechanistic evidence; document uncertainty and rationale
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: Direct evidence for this drug–RT combination is insufficient. Specialist review and documented multidisciplinary reasoning are recommended.
No major interaction signal identified; proceed with standard clinical checks and clinician override.

Interaction_Rules.RT_site_or_OAR_trigger: Any; heightened concern for central thorax, mucosa, bowel or marrow-rich fields
Interaction_Rules.Required_action: First determine whether chemotherapy is an intentional validated radiosensitising regimen. Outside a protocol-standard concurrent chemoradiation regimen, avoid concomitant cytotoxic chemotherapy with SABR/SBRT or SRS. Consider withholding chemotherapy for approximately 1–3 days before and after RT, adjusted where necessary for the individual agent, half-life, dosing schedule, irradiated site, marrow reserve and clinical urgency.
Interaction_Rules.Senior_RO_review: Recommended for central/ultracentral, visceral, marrow-rich or uncertain combinations
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: Outside a validated concurrent chemoradiation regimen, concomitant cytotoxic chemotherapy should generally be avoided with SABR/SBRT or SRS because high-dose-per-fraction RT may amplify focal normal-tissue or marrow toxicity. Consider an approximately 1–3-day interval before and after RT, individualised to the agent and clinical context.

Interaction_Rules.RT_site_or_OAR_trigger: Any; especially large central thoracic, mucosal, bowel or marrow-rich fields
Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Individualise according to target volume, OAR exposure, urgency, systemic disease trajectory and consequences of interrupting chemotherapy. Small peripheral targets may be treated concurrently; large central or mucosal fields warrant greater caution.
Interaction_Rules.Senior_RO_review: If large field, central thorax, substantial bowel/mucosal exposure or severe cytopenia
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: Concurrent cytotoxic therapy during palliative RT is not automatically contraindicated; risk depends strongly on field size, site and clinical urgency.

Interaction_Rules.Agent_or_class_trigger: Multikinase tyrosine kinase inhibitor (for example sorafenib, sunitinib, pazopanib or cabozantinib)
Interaction_Rules.Required_action: Review the specific agent and half-life. The Sanford framework suggests holding during RT and considering an approximately 3-day pre-RT washout; do not apply this automatically where stronger agent-specific evidence recommends a different interval.
Interaction_Rules.Senior_RO_review: Recommended for SABR/SRS, critical OAR overlap or uncertain evidence
Interaction_Rules.Output_warning_template: Concurrent multikinase inhibition may increase site-specific toxicity; timing should be individualised using pharmacology and stronger evidence where available.

Australian_Medicine_Master.Highest-concern RT sites / OARs: Site-dependent OARs only
Australian_Medicine_Master.Fractionation-specific warning: No specific fractionation signal identified; use standard site-specific RT caution
Australian_Medicine_Master.Timing-specific warning: Concurrent use usually acceptable if clinically indicated; verify PI/protocol
Australian_Medicine_Master.Medical Oncology discussion: No routine extra discussion unless site/fractionation or patient factors increase risk
Australian_Medicine_Master.Senior RO review: No routine senior RO review beyond standard practice

Conventional/palliative RT generally CAUTION with MedOnc discussion.

Australian_Medicine_Master.RT toxicity domains: Pneumonitis, colitis/enteritis, hepatitis, dermatitis, endocrinopathy, inflammatory flare/radiation recall
Australian_Medicine_Master.Highest-concern RT sites / OARs: Lung, bowel/rectum, liver, skin, brain oedema/radionecrosis context
Australian_Medicine_Master.Fractionation-specific warning: Central lung SBRT concurrent = HIGH RISK (grade ≥3 pneumonitis 10.7%); ultracentral lung SBRT = AVOID concurrent; oesophageal SBRT = HIGH RISK; upper abdominal SABR (liver/pancreas) = HIGH RISK; pelvic SBRT = HIGH RISK; conventional lung CRT concurrent = MODERATE CONCERN (KEYNOTE-001: 13% vs 1% pneumonitis); durvalumab post-CRT ≤42 days = CAUTION/standard of care (PACIFIC trial).
Australian_Medicine_Master.Timing-specific warning: Assess recent, concurrent and planned immunotherapy; monitor for immune-mediated toxicity
Australian_Medicine_Master.Medical Oncology discussion: Yes for thorax/abdomen/pelvis/brain SRS or symptomatic immune toxicity
Australian_Medicine_Master.Senior RO review: Yes for high-dose thoracic RT, brain SRS or reirradiation contexts
Australian_Medicine_Master.App warning / documentation text: Atezolizumab (anti-PD-L1): HIGH RISK with central lung SBRT, ultracentral (AVOID), oesophageal SBRT, upper abdominal/pelvic SABR.

Australian_Medicine_Master.RT toxicity domains: DNA repair radiosensitisation, marrow suppression, GI toxicity
Australian_Medicine_Master.Highest-concern RT sites / OARs: Lung (radical RT), spine/bone SABR, brain SRS (olaparib)
Australian_Medicine_Master.Fractionation-specific warning: SABR/SBRT at any site = HIGH RISK (potent PARP-trapping → severe DNA-repair impairment); radical lung RT (conventional 60–66 Gy) = HIGH RISK; spine/bone SABR = HIGH RISK; brain SRS = CAUTION. Talazoparib is the most potent PARP trapper — HIGH RISK at virtually all sites.
Australian_Medicine_Master.Timing-specific warning: Discuss hold interval for pelvic/abdominal/spine or large-field RT
Australian_Medicine_Master.Medical Oncology discussion: Yes for SABR/SBRT, radical thoracic RT or high-dose pelvic RT
Australian_Medicine_Master.Senior RO review: Yes for SABR/SBRT, radical lung RT or spine/bone SABR
Australian_Medicine_Master.App warning / documentation text: Niraparib + RT: PARP-trapping mechanism radiosensitises normal tissue. HIGH RISK with SABR/SBRT or radical lung RT. Senior RO review for any SABR combination.
Australian_Medicine_Master.Verification status: Unverified first-pass inventory: reconcile against ARTG/PBS/eviQ before clinical release
Australian_Medicine_Master.Notes: Include brand/ARTG/PBS details after official reconciliation

Australian_Medicine_Master.RT toxicity domains: General overlapping toxicity only
Australian_Medicine_Master.Highest-concern RT sites / OARs: Brain (SRS), lung (SABR/central), liver (conventional/SABR)
Australian_Medicine_Master.Fractionation-specific warning: Brain SRS concurrent = HIGH RISK (all vinca alkaloids); lung central/ultracentral SABR = HIGH RISK; lung peripheral SABR = HIGH RISK; liver/upper abdomen conventional = AVOID concurrent (especially vincristine, vinblastine); conventional lung/H&N/spine = MODERATE CONCERN; palliative bone RT = LOW CONCERN.
Australian_Medicine_Master.Timing-specific warning: Concurrent use usually acceptable if clinically indicated; verify PI/protocol
Australian_Medicine_Master.Medical Oncology discussion: Yes for brain SRS, SABR, or any high-dose concurrent RT
Australian_Medicine_Master.Senior RO review: Yes for brain SRS, lung SABR, liver RT
Australian_Medicine_Master.App warning / documentation text: Vinblastine (vinca alkaloid): MODERATE CONCERN. HIGH RISK with brain SRS, lung SABR (peripheral or central), liver conventional RT. AVOID concurrent upper abdominal RT (hepatotoxicity). Senior RO review for SRS, SABR, or liver RT.

Australian_Medicine_Master.RT toxicity domains: Mucositis, pneumonitis, dermatitis, impaired healing, marrow suppression
Australian_Medicine_Master.Highest-concern RT sites / OARs: Lung (all RT, especially SABR), liver SABR, pelvis/bone SABR, H&N
Australian_Medicine_Master.Fractionation-specific warning: Lung RT (conventional radical or SABR) = HIGH RISK (everolimus/temsirolimus pneumonitis + radiation pneumonitis additive); lung SABR peripheral = HIGH RISK; lung SABR central/ultracentral = HIGH RISK; liver SABR = HIGH RISK (RILD + mTOR hepatotoxicity); pelvis/bone SABR = HIGH RISK; brain GBM conventional = MODERATE CONCERN; brain SRS = CAUTION.
Australian_Medicine_Master.Timing-specific warning: Consider hold interval for high-risk sites
Australian_Medicine_Master.Medical Oncology discussion: Mandatory for any thoracic RT or SABR
Australian_Medicine_Master.Senior RO review: Mandatory for any thoracic RT or SABR at any extracranial site
Australian_Medicine_Master.App warning / documentation text: Everolimus (mTOR inhibitor): HIGH-RISK with thoracic RT (all fractionations) and SABR at any site. mTOR-inhibitor pneumonitis + radiation pneumonitis are additive and potentially fatal. Senior RO + Med Onc review mandatory for any SABR or radical thoracic RT.

High_Risk_Watchlist.Fractionation-specific warning: High caution with SABR/ablative RT near bowel, bronchus, oesophagus, vessels or surgical wounds
High_Risk_Watchlist.Timing-specific warning: Avoid concurrent where possible; discuss hold interval before/after RT, especially invasive sites
High_Risk_Watchlist.Senior RO review: Yes for SABR/hypofractionation or hollow viscus/central thorax
High_Risk_Watchlist.App warning / documentation text: Anti-VEGF/VEGFR or multikinase therapy may increase bleeding, fistula/perforation and wound-healing risk with RT, especially near hollow viscus or central thoracic structures.
High_Risk_Watchlist.Verification status: Unverified first-pass inventory: reconcile against ARTG/PBS/eviQ before clinical release
High_Risk_Watchlist.Notes: Include brand/ARTG/PBS details after official reconciliation

RT_Interaction_Logic.Default RT concern: Strong radiosensitiser with severe normal tissue toxicity reports
RT_Interaction_Logic.Key OARs/sites: Lung, oesophagus, bowel, liver, skin, marrow
RT_Interaction_Logic.Fractionation issue: Avoid SABR/hypofractionated concurrent use unless justified
RT_Interaction_Logic.Default action: Mandatory Med Onc and senior RO review

RT_Interaction_Logic.Default RT concern: Bleeding, fistula, perforation, wound healing, vascular toxicity
RT_Interaction_Logic.Key OARs/sites: Bowel, oesophagus, bronchus, bladder/rectum, vessels, wounds
RT_Interaction_Logic.Fractionation issue: High caution with SABR near hollow viscus/central thorax/vessels
RT_Interaction_Logic.Default action: Mandatory Med Onc review for high-risk sites

RT_Interaction_Logic.Default RT concern: Pneumonitis, colitis, hepatitis, dermatitis, inflammatory toxicity
RT_Interaction_Logic.Fractionation issue: Caution with thoracic RT, abdominal/pelvic RT, brain SRS/SABR

RT_Interaction_Logic.Default RT concern: Payload-specific toxicity including marrow, mucosa, skin, GI, hepatic, ILD/pneumonitis for selected drugs
RT_Interaction_Logic.Key OARs/sites: Lung, marrow, mucosa, bowel, skin, liver
RT_Interaction_Logic.Fractionation issue: Caution with SABR/hypofractionation and large fields
RT_Interaction_Logic.Default action: Verify payload-specific PI and discuss with Med Onc

RT_Interaction_Logic.Default RT concern: DNA repair inhibition and potential radiosensitisation
RT_Interaction_Logic.Fractionation issue: Caution with pelvic/abdominal/spine RT, SABR and large fields

RT_Interaction_Logic.Default RT concern: Marrow, GI, skin and possible lung toxicity overlap
RT_Interaction_Logic.Fractionation issue: Caution with pelvis, spine, thorax and large fields
RT_Interaction_Logic.Timing issue: Consider holding around RT depending on context

RT_Interaction_Logic.Default RT concern: Pneumonitis, mucositis, impaired healing, marrow toxicity
RT_Interaction_Logic.Fractionation issue: Caution with thoracic/mucosal RT and hypofractionation

RT_Interaction_Logic.Default RT concern: Mucositis, dermatitis, oesophagitis, pneumonitis/ILD depending agent
